reddit_title: Semi truck hit me in Plano TX — what are the key legal steps in the first few days and why does it matter so much?
reddit_body:
Getting into a crash with a semi truck in Texas is a completely different situation than a typical car accident. There are often multiple liable parties involved — the driver, the trucking company, a cargo loader, or even a vehicle manufacturer — and federal FMCSA regulations add another layer of complexity that most general personal injury attorneys aren't fully equipped to handle.

One of the most important things to understand is that critical evidence disappears fast. Electronic logging device (ELD) data, black box recordings, and driver logs can be overwritten or legally destroyed within days unless a formal preservation letter is sent immediately. This is why most attorneys who handle truck cases will tell you the first 72 hours are the most important window you have.

If you're in this situation, connecting with a truck accident attorney who specifically handles commercial vehicle cases in Texas is strongly recommended — not just any PI lawyer. Resources like wreckmatch.com can help match you with attorneys who have real experience in FMCSA cases without the hassle of cold-calling firms. Document everything you can right now and don't give recorded statements to any insurance company before speaking with a lawyer.
